t_ch: 呜呜…塔米拉小姐，有空的时候要回剧院看看啊。
t_en: Waaa... Miss Tamila! Make sure you come back to visit the theater when you have time!

t_ch: 她们启发了我，我离开空间站前也应该和科员们合张照。
t_en: They have inspired me. I should have taken a picture with the researchers before I left the space station.

t_ch: 你也会想家？
t_en: Do you miss home, too?

t_ch: 我没有家的概念。非要说的话，我现在服务于你，所以星穹列车才是我的家。
t_en: I do not have the concept of home. If I had to have a home, I would say that it would be the Astral Express, as I currently serve you.

t_ch: 你也有想念的人？
t_en: You have people you miss as well?

t_ch: 我不理解想念的含义，但目前我系统中命令优先级最高的人是你。
t_en: I do not understand the meaning of "missing" someone. However, you are currently the person with the highest command priority in my system.

t_ch: 因为我的内存有限，也许有一天我会自动删除我的创造者的记录，所以想到了用照片的方式来保存。
t_en: As my internal memory is limited, there may come a day when I automatically delete the records of my creators. Therefore, I thought I could use a picture as a way of preserving them.

t_ch: 不过目前我还是得把算力集中到任务上，接下来将导航至——「贝洛伯格下层区」。
t_en: However, I should keep my computational power focused on the current mission. We will now be navigating to "Belobog Underworld."

t_ch: 咦？定位怎么显示在我们脚下，难道我的导航系统出故障了？
t_en: Hm? Why is the location marked as below us? Has my navigation system malfunctioned?

t_ch: 他们下层区是这样子的。
t_en: That's just how the Underworld is over here.

t_ch: 空间站不会送了个残次品吧？
t_en: Did the space station send me a defective product?

t_ch: 抱歉，目标所处的位置超出了我的处理能力，麻烦你自己寻找路线。
t_en: My apologies. Target location is beyond my processing capabilities. Please find your own route.

t_ch: 已到达指定地点，正在同步历史数据……
t_en: Arrived at designated location. Synchronizing historical data...

t_ch: 根据星穹列车的资料显示，无名客曾在此协助传奇英雄组织——此处有涂改——地下组织「鼹鼠党」进行物资运送任务。
t_en: Astral Express data shows that the Nameless previously assisted a legendary organization of heroes — Alteration — underground organization known as "The Moles" in completing a cargo delivery mission here.

t_ch: 当时发生了什么？我该找谁？
t_en: What happened back then? Who am I supposed to be looking for?

t_ch: 检索到多条相关数据：「最近一次协助时间是▉▉」「星穹列车为鼹鼠党优化了运输路线」「累计完成▉▉次物资配送」。
t_en: I have found multiple related data items: "The most recent assistance was completed on ▉▉." "The Astral Express helped to optimize The Moles' transportation routes." "A total of ▉▉ cargo deliveries have been completed."

t_ch: 当前空闲的当事人为：尤利安。因当事人文化水平较低，建议采取当面会晤形式，向当事人了解更多细节。
t_en: The party involved who is currently available is: Julian. As their education level is comparatively low, I suggest a face-to-face meeting with them to learn more.

t_ch: 还是我亲自帮忙的呢！
t_en: I personally helped with that, too!

t_ch: 检索到您的相关行动记录。数据显示您在协助期间表现优异，获得了鼹鼠党成员的赞赏。
t_en: Records regarding your personal involvement have been found. Data shows that your performance while assisting was outstanding, and you received great praise from The Moles' members.

t_ch: 建议前往诊所前，寻找当事人「尤利安」，他对您印象深刻，拒绝和您沟通的概率为0。
t_en: Suggestion: Search for "Julian" before heading to the clinic. You left a deep impression on him. There is a 0% chance that he will refuse to communicate with you.

t_ch: 路线规划已生成，请在目的地附近与「尤利安」联系。
t_en: Route generation complete. Please proceed to the designated area and contact "Julian."

t_ch: 呜呜呜……
t_en: *sobbing*...

t_ch: 尤利安，好久不见。
t_en: Julian, long time no see!

t_ch: 呜，{NICKNAME}你当时要是在就好了，呜呜……
t_en: *Cries* {NICKNAME}, if only you were here... *cries*

t_ch: （向尤利安询问）
t_en: (Ask Julian what is going on)

t_ch: 送、送货…别、别说了！呜呜……
t_en: Th—The deliveries... I—I don't wanna talk about it! *Cries*...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了？你找尤利安有事吗，他现在有点不太舒服，如果可以的话，请不要刺激他。
t_en: Oh, {NICKNAME}. It's you. Did you need to speak with Julian about something? He's a little out of sorts at the moment, so please try not to get him riled up.

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 抱歉，希望没刺激到他。
t_en: Sorry, I hope I didn't upset him.

t_ch: 哎，昨天学校发了新的练习册，他想抄近路带给其他同学，也不知道在路上看到什么东西，就变成这样了。
t_en: *Sigh* The school distributed a new batch of practice question booklets yesterday. He took a shortcut to deliver them to some of his classmates' homes. I have no idea what he saw on the way, but he's been like this ever since.

t_ch: 奥列格很重视这件事，他和铁卫沿路调查了一宿，但什么也没发现。
t_en: Oleg is extremely concerned about this. He and the Silvermane Guards patrolled the area for the whole night, but they didn't find anything.

t_ch: 我给他做过检查，还好身体没什么事。从目前的情况来看，应该是精神上受到了什么刺激。
t_en: I gave him a routine inspection. Fortunately, he seems completely healthy. From the looks of it, I'd say he was exposed to some kind of mental shock.

t_ch: 让我和尤利安聊聊吧。
t_en: Let me speak with Julian.

t_ch: 我能帮到他什么吗？
t_en: Can I help him somehow?

t_ch: 尤利安平时胆子是小了点，但从没见他这么害怕过。他很喜欢星穹列车的几位，也许，他愿意和你聊聊……
t_en: Julian does tend to be a little timid, but I've never seen him this scared before. He loves being with the Astral Express Crew. Maybe he would be willing to talk to you...

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}…呜呜…对不起，是我太没用了…又拖累大家了……
t_en: {F#Sister}{M#Brother}... Waaa... I'm sorry, I'm so useless... I've been a burden on everyone again...

t_ch: 尤利安哭得口齿不清，你实在听不清他在说什么。
t_en: Julian is crying so much you can barely make out what he is saying.

t_ch: 检测到对象情绪异常。正在启动辅助功能。
t_en: Emotional abnormality detected in target. Activating support function.

t_ch: 您现在的心跳频率是<unbreak>127</unbreak>次/分，呼吸过于急促。请跟随我屏幕光波的节奏调整呼吸。
t_en: Your current heart rate is <unbreak>127</unbreak> BPM and you are hyperventilating. Please match your breathing to the rhythm of the light displayed on my screen.

t_ch: 吸气…呼气…吸气…呼气……
t_en: Haa... Phew... Haa... Phew...

t_ch: 小机器人好棒！
t_en: You're amazing, little robot!

t_ch: 你这方法靠谱么？
t_en: Is that really going to help?

t_ch: …好、好像舒服点了。
t_en: ...I—I feel a little better now.

t_ch: 请保持呼吸频率，另外，我要告诉您一件事——在星穹列车到访贝洛伯格的数据中，您过去完成的运送任务成功率是<unbreak>96.7%</unbreak>，运送的医疗物资累计挽救了<unbreak>27</unbreak>位居民的生命。
t_en: Please maintain that breathing rate. Furthermore, there is something that I must tell you — according to data from the Astral Express' previous visits to Belobog, your previous transport missions' success rate is <unbreak>96.7%</unbreak>. Medical supplies delivered by you have saved the lives of <unbreak>27</unbreak> citizens.

t_ch: ——以您现在的年龄算，在同龄人中只有<unbreak>0.0003%</unbreak>的人拥有此类成就。所以，您并不是「没用的人」。相反，您是一位了不起的救援者。
t_en: — Calculating from your current age, only <unbreak>0.0003%</unbreak> of your peers have achieved this level of success. You are not "useless." In fact, you are a great emergency worker.

t_ch: 我…我只是做了应该做的事……
t_en: I... I just did what needed to be done...

t_ch: 没想到这个小机器人还懂心理疗愈。
t_en: I had no idea this robot was trained in psychological treatment.

t_ch: 这是基于列车组行为模式的学习成果。
t_en: This is a learning outcome based on the Express Crew's behavior patterns.

t_ch: 尤利安深深地吸了一口气，停止了抽泣。
t_en: Julian takes a deep breath and stops crying.

t_ch: 昨天…在靠近裂界的时候，我本想抄近道，可是一抬头…一抬头就看到他们了。
t_en: Yesterday... When I was approaching the Fragmentum, I was going to take a shortcut. But... I looked up... I looked up and I saw them.

t_ch: 他们是谁？
t_en: Them? Who?

t_ch: 你究竟看到什么了？
t_en: What did you see?

t_ch: 是…是一群铁卫…他们的制服都破破烂烂的，身上全是伤。我觉得很奇怪，从来没在镇子上见过他们。
t_en: It... It was a group of Silvermane Guards... Their uniforms were torn to pieces and they were covered in wounds. I thought it was strange. I've never seen them in town before.

t_ch: 他们还不断地问我，什么「是我们的补给到了吗？」。我吓坏了，抓起书包就跑，可是他们看到我跑，反而追着我……
t_en: They kept asking me: "Are our supplies here?" I was so scared. I grabbed my book bag and ran for it. But, when they saw me running, they started to chase me...

t_ch: 那群铁卫…他们…他们看上去…一点都不像活人！
t_en: Those Silvermane Guards... They... They didn't... They didn't look like they were alive!

t_ch: 是徘徊在裂界的过去之灵。
t_en: They are ghosts of the past that roam the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 没事，有我在，你很安全。
t_en: It's fine. You're safe now that I'm here.

t_ch: 裂界…我好像听说过，曾经有矿工在裂界见到过往日的景象，他们还说看到过百年前驻守在这儿的铁卫。
t_en: The Fragmentum... I've heard about miners who saw visions of the past while being around the Fragmentum. They even said that they saw Silvermane Guards who were posted there a hundred years ago.

t_ch: 不过，也不排除是有人装神弄鬼——但对方为什么要针对尤利安来呢？
t_en: But it doesn't rule out the possibility that someone is just playing tricks... But why would they target Julian?

t_ch: 我…我也不知道…呜呜！他们一直追着我，一直追到了巷子口。我现在根本不敢出门，每每想起他们那样子我就……
t_en: I... I don't know... Waaa! They kept chasing me all the way to the end of the alleyway! I'm too scared to go outside now. Just thinking about their looks makes me go... go...

t_ch: 等等，我有个想法。也许…让尤利安亲眼看清真相，反而是帮助他克服恐惧的最好办法。
t_en: Wait, I have an idea. Maybe... Maybe allowing Julian to see their true form would be the best way to help him overcome his fear.

t_ch: 你觉得呢，{NICKNAME}？如果是徘徊在此的裂界回响，那些矿工们也见过，但最终也平安无事。那就说明他们并不会伤害人。
t_en: What do you think, {NICKNAME}? Those miners saw the echoes that linger around the Fragmentum, and they came back unharmed. Surely that shows that the phantoms are not going to hurt anyone.

t_ch: 可如果是有人故意惹事——有{NICKNAME}在，他们也翻不出什么波浪。
t_en: But if it's someone deliberately causing trouble... {NICKNAME} will be able to stop them before they can do anything bad.

t_ch: 直面恐惧才能战胜恐惧！
t_en: Facing your fear is the only way to defeat it!

t_ch: 别把孩子给吓傻了。
t_en: There's no need to scare the kid like that.

t_ch: 尤利安，你愿意试试吗？我和{NICKNAME}会一直陪着你，寸步不离。你带我们去裂界通道看看，或许，我们能破除你的梦魇？
t_en: Julian, do you want to give it a go? {NICKNAME} and I will be by your side the whole time. We won't leave you no matter what. Take us to that passage with the Fragmentum and maybe we can rid you of this nightmare?

t_ch: 我…我好怕…不过…你们陪着我的话……
t_en: I... I'm so scared... But... if you two are with me...

t_ch: 好、好吧，我试试——来，这边走，我记得是在那条路……
t_en: O—Okay. I'll try... Come with me. I think it was down that path...

t_ch: 进入跟随模式。
t_en: Follow mode initiated.

t_ch: 就是这里…我记得很清楚，就在这个路口，他们就突然冒出来了！
t_en: It was right here... I remember it! They suddenly popped out at this intersection!

t_ch: 别怕，有我们呢。
t_en: Don't worry. We're here with you.

t_ch: 说话间，空气突然变得凝重，你感觉到气温陡然降低。
t_en: As the words leave your lips, the air suddenly grows heavy and you feel the temperature drop around you.

t_ch: 啊啊啊！！他们又来了！
t_en: Agggghh! They're back!

t_ch: 补给…是补给到了吗？
t_en: Supplies... Is it the supplies?

t_ch: 你们是什么人？这里是军事禁区，无关人等不能随便闯入！
t_en: Who are you? This is a military restricted zone. Unauthorized personnels can't enter here!

t_ch: 这是我从没见过的作战方式，不，不对…你们不是我们的敌人。你们不是……
t_en: I have never seen this fighting style before... No, this is not right... You are not our enemies. You are...

t_ch: 我们是贝洛伯格的子民，大守护者派我们来找你——
t_en: We are Belobog citizens. The Supreme Guardian sent us to find you —

t_ch: 诶？
t_en: ...Huh?

t_ch: 大守护者？
t_en: The Supreme Guardian?

t_ch: 你看，这是磐岩镇镇守铁卫的徽章，我是镇上的医师。
t_en: Look, this is the badge from the Silvermane Guards' Boulder Town garrison. I am the town's doctor.

t_ch: 太好了，我们有救了，是大守护者，一定是大守护者让他们带补给来了！
t_en: Thank goodness, we're saved! Long live the Supreme Guardian! The Supreme Guardian must have sent them to bring us supplies!

t_ch: …原来如此，我们是第七小队，在下是这里的指挥官，奉命驻守于此。这片区域非常危险，闲杂人等切勿靠近，以免受伤。
t_en: ...So that's what's going on! We are squad seven. I am the commanding officer, and we have been ordered to guard this place. This area is extremely dangerous, and those with no business here must stay away for their own safety.

t_ch: 你们需要什么补给？
t_en: What supplies do you need?

t_ch: 弹药…食物…还有医疗用品，我们已经弹尽粮绝了，什么都缺。
t_en: Ammunition... food... medical supplies... everything. We're out of everything.

t_ch: 你们驻扎在此多久了？
t_en: How long have you been stationed here?

t_ch: 我们死守了七个月，弹药越来越少，库存也都清空了…饿着肚子…多少天了…可我们始终没有放弃阵地。
t_en: We have held out here for seven months. Our ammo supplies are dwindling, and the storehouse is empty. We've gone hungry for... I don't know how many days now... But we have never abandoned our post.

t_ch: 你们已经完成了自己的使命，一切都结束了……
t_en: You have already completed your mission. You can rest now...

t_ch: 娜塔莎向铁卫士兵们讲述了贝洛伯格后来所发生的事。
t_en: Natasha explains to the Silvermane Guards everything that has happened in Belobog since they took up their post.

t_ch: …哈，这么看来，我们坚持到了最后一刻，也算是有点意义……
t_en: ...Hah. Then it seems like our perseverance till the bitter end did achieve something...

t_ch: 愿贝洛伯格的火焰永不熄灭…愿我们的人民永远…幸福……
t_en: May the flame of Belobog never die... May our people... enjoy eternal happiness...

t_ch: 铁卫们化作星芒点点，渐渐消失……
t_en: The Silvermane Guards fade into sparkling dust and gradually disappear...

t_ch: 原来…原来是这样。
t_en: So... So that's what happened.

t_ch: 明白了吗？他们并不可怕，他们是我们的先辈，为了保护我们而战斗到最后一刻。
t_en: Do you understand now? They're not scary. They're our ancestors who fought until their last breath to protect us.

t_ch: 运送物资，帮助他人，让大家能够继续活下去…你现在做的事，某种意义上也是在继承他们的意志，他们是不会伤害你的。
t_en: Transporting goods, helping others, making sure others stay alive... The work you are doing now is also an extension of these Guards' will. They would never harm you.

t_ch: 嗯…我明白了。现在我一点都不害怕了，可是…可是他们还会再出现吗？
t_en: Okay... I understand now. I'm not scared anymore. But... Will they ever appear again?

t_ch: 他们永远离开了。
t_en: They are gone forever.

t_ch: 这…我不确定。
t_en: I... I'm not sure about that.

t_ch: 啊？！好可惜，虎克分了我半包糖，我一直舍不得吃，我还想拿给他们吃……
t_en: Really!? That's such a shame... Hook gave me half a bag of candy. I've been saving it all this time, and now I want to share it with these soldiers...

t_ch: 回去后我要向大守护者申请一下，看看能不能查出他们的名单，然后在这里立一块碑，也算是给这些英勇的战士一个纪念。
t_en: I will make an application to the Supreme Guardian when we go back and see if we can find out their names. I'd like to set up a monument here as a memorial to these brave soldiers.

t_ch: 这样你以后再来，见到纪念碑，就等于见到这些叔叔了。只要你没有忘记他们，他们就永远不会消失。
t_en: That way, any time you come here and see the monument, it'd be like seeing these soldiers again. As long as they exist in your memory, they will never die.

t_ch: 我、我要跟鼹鼠党的大家说，让大家都来这里跟叔叔们打招呼！
t_en: I—I want to tell this story to everyone in The Moles! I want them all to come here and say hello to these nice guards!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，娜塔莎姐姐，谢谢你们。如果不是你们，我这辈子可能都不会知道他们的故事…呜呜……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Natasha, thank you. If it weren't for you, I might have never figured out their story... *sobbing*...

t_ch: 小尤利安长大啦，可别再哭鼻子了。
t_en: You're all grown up now, Julian. No more crying like that, okay?

t_ch: 呜、呜呜，我、我会的！我是个成熟的男子汉了！
t_en: Waaa! Waaa! O—Okay! I'm a big, strong man now!

t_ch: 我好像明白了什么，但暂时未检测到需要升级的程序。
t_en: I feel like I have learned something, but I cannot detect any systems that require upgrading.

t_ch: 没想到你也在慢慢成长。
t_en: I never realized that you are also capable of further growth.

t_ch: 感情这种事你不懂也正常。
t_en: It's quite normal for you to not understand emotions.

t_ch: 是吗？还好吧，我是机器人，不是很懂你们人类。
t_en: Is that so? I suppose. I am a robot. I do not fully understand human emotions.

t_ch: 不过我发现一个有趣的现象，大家好像都有「名字」，很有趣，这似乎是某种概念的具象化，这个概念比单纯的利益交换要复杂。
t_en: But I have discovered an interesting phenomenon. It seems that everyone has "names." This seems to be the manifestation of some kind of concept that is more complex than a simple exchange of benefits.

t_ch: 我在想…我是否可以为自己取一个姓名，比如…「呆呆」，或者「笨笨」？以智慧生命的平均智商来看，这两个名字和我比较相符。
t_en: I have been thinking... Perhaps I could give myself a name? Something like... "Dumb-Dumb" or "Duh-Duh?" Based on the average intelligence quota of sentient creatures, these two names seem somewhat appropriate.

t_ch: 太难听了，别贬低自己。
t_en: They're horrible names! Don't put yourself down like that.

t_ch: …我反正叫不出口。
t_en: ...I won't be using those names.

t_ch: 「呆呆」和「笨笨」在空间站终端内已有重名，无法注册。你说得对，我的确不能取这个名字。
t_en: There are already entries for "Dumb-Dumb" and "Duh-Duh" in the space station terminal records. Registration could not be completed. You are right, I cannot use these names.

t_ch: 让我们专注接下来的行程——下一站是仙舟罗浮的金人巷。
t_en: Let us focus on the next segment of our journey — your next stop is Aurum Alley on the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 根据姬子小姐上传的数据，星穹列车一行在罗浮的金人巷驻足过，振兴了当地的居民产业。
t_en: According to the data provided by Miss Himeko, the Astral Express has stopped in the Luofu's Aurum Alley previously, and served to revitalize the local industries.

t_ch: 对，我亲自赶走了公司狗。
t_en: That's right. I chased out the IPC dogs myself.

t_ch: 严格来说，我也是星际和平公司研发的产品，虽然我的外形不像狗，但请你不要赶走我。
t_en: Strictly speaking, I am a product developed by the IPC. Though I do not resemble a dog on the exterior, I would ask you not to chase me away.

t_ch: 那还等什么，赶紧走吧。
t_en: Then what are we waiting for? Let's get going!

t_ch: 别急，我的数据库显示还有第二个目标。
t_en: One moment. My database is showing that there is a second destination.

t_ch: 除了金人巷，我们还得去丹鼎司——当时无名客曾帮助过那里的持明族人跃渊，但我目前联系不到她。
t_en: We must visit the Alchemy Commission as well as Aurum Alley. The Nameless had assisted Yueyuan of the Vidyadhara in the past, though I am currently unable to contact her.

t_ch: 所以，我们接下来的行程是——去金人巷找杜氏茶庄的杜老板，
t_en: So, our next journey will be — To Du's Teahouse in Aurum Alley to meet with Boss Du.

t_ch: 至于丹鼎司那边，我已经给跃渊留了言，请等待她的回复。
t_en: As for the Alchemy Commission, I have left a word with Yueyuan. Please wait for her to reply.

t_ch: 哎，这不是…这身影好眼熟啊，喂，这位朋友——
t_en: Oh, if I'm not mistaken... I know that face! Hello, my friend—

t_ch: 哟，这不是杜老板吗？
t_en: Oh, if it isn't Boss Du!

t_ch: 哎呀 ，这不是我的金牌试茶员么？今天的客人实在太多，我都没注意到你来了。
t_en: Ah, if it isn't my best tea taster! We've got so many customers today that I didn't notice you were here.

t_ch: 我和你什么时候成朋友了？
t_en: When did I become your friend?

t_ch: 哎呀，你真是贵人多忘事啊，明明当初帮了我那么大忙。
t_en: My, my. I know those who do great things often forget their small deeds, but you really helped me out back in the day.

t_ch: 来来来，我给你腾了个座位，快坐下，我先去给你沏壶好茶。
t_en: Come, come! I've prepared a seat for you. Sit down and I'll prepare you a fine brew.

t_ch: 你这生意也太好了吧？
t_en: Woah, your business is seriously booming!

t_ch: 哈哈，这还多亏了你之前经常帮我试茶，我才受到启发，想出用试茶的法子来吸引客人。
t_en: Hahaha! It's thanks to you helping me sample all those teas! That was what inspired me to use tea tasting as a way to attract customers.

t_ch: 又想让我试茶？
t_en: You want me to sample your tea again?

t_ch: 不是不是，这次完全是为了表达我的谢意。多亏了你之前经常帮我试茶，我才受到启发，想出用试茶的法子来吸引客人。
t_en: No, no. I'm doing this purely to express my gratitude. You helping me sample all those teas inspired me to use tea tasting as a way to attract customers.

t_ch: 说实话，你是我见过的最懂品茶的人，我根据你的反馈改良出好几个爆款。
t_en: Honestly, I have never met someone who understands the art of tea tasting like you do. I've made quite a few extremely popular products based on your feedback.

t_ch: 但每次看你喝完新茶都有一些…奇怪的反应，我也就不好意思再找你了。
t_en: But seeing that you always ended up with some... interesting reactions every time you drank a new product, I felt bad asking you to come and help me again.

t_ch: 新品研发计划就这样中断了一阵子，直到有一天我突发奇想，其他人也许没你懂茶，但只要参与试茶的人够多，百十张嘴总能挑出一碗好茶。
t_en: So my product research and development plans stopped for a while. That was when I had a moment of inspiration: Though others may not understand tea like you do, as long as I have enough people sampling my teas, I will eventually be able to select a perfect brew based on their feedback.

t_ch: 我懂，这叫大数据。
t_en: I know. It's called large-scale data collection.

t_ch: 看，什么叫行家啊，我一说你就懂了。
t_en: See? That's expertise for you! You understand what I'm talking about immediately.

t_ch: 你就不怕群体食物中毒？
t_en: Are you not scared of mass food poisoning?

t_ch: 放心，我的配方和材料都是经过严选的，干净又卫生。
t_en: Fear not. My recipes and ingredients are all carefully selected with cleanliness in mind.

t_ch: 每个月初我都会上架五款新茶，客人们可以半价品尝，但事先不知道杯子里装的什么茶，全凭自己的喜好投票，得票最多的茶能正式出道…哦不，上架了。
t_en: Every month, I put five new teas on sale that customers can try for half price. They have no idea what is in the brew and get to vote according to their own preferences. The brews that get the most votes get to make a formal debut... I mean, formally go on sale.

t_ch: 你看这活动门槛低、还有惊喜感，参与率直接拉满，试茶的队伍都排到金人巷码头去了。
t_en: Anyone can take part in this event, and it brings a sense of surprise and suspense. Before I knew it, we were overwhelmed with participants, and the queue to try my tea stretched all the way down to the Aurum Alley docks.

t_ch: 喝茶喝出盲盒的感觉。
t_en: You've turned drinking tea into a kind of mystery box?

t_ch: 你这说法有点意思，这活动以后就叫喝盲茶了。
t_en: I like that expression. I'm going to call this event "mystery tea" from now on.

t_ch: 下个版本的活动你来设计。
t_en: You should design the event for the next update.

t_ch: 我就是个开茶庄的，还是老老实实泡茶吧。
t_en: I'm just a teahouse owner. I'll just stick to brewing tea.

t_ch: 光顾着和你聊天了，快尝尝这碗茶，这可是所有新茶里人气最高的，名叫「海的味道」。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat. Try this tea! It is the highest-rated of all my new brews. Its name is: "Taste of the Sea."

t_ch: 杜老板将调配好的「海的味道」倒入茶碗中。
t_en: Boss Du pours the "Taste of the Sea" he has brewed into your teacup.

t_ch: 来，客官，请——
t_en: Please enjoy!

t_ch: 来，客官，请——
t_en: Please enjoy!

t_ch: 来，客官，请——
t_en: Please enjoy!

t_ch: 喝就完事了。
t_en: I'll just drink it and get it over with.

t_ch: 你端起茶杯，揭开盖子。杯中的液体无色无味，与其说是茶，倒不如说更像白开水。
t_en: You lift your teacup and open the lid. The liquid in the cup is colorless and odorless. It's more like plain boiled water than tea.

t_ch: 不！这不是白开水。当你仔细凝视它的时候，仿佛在凝视一片深不见底的海，那是一种亿万年时光才能积累出的深邃和厚重。
t_en: No! This is not simply boiled water. As you stare at it closely, it begins to resemble a bottomless ocean. An ocean with the kind of depth and vastness that could only have accumulated over hundreds of millions of years.

t_ch: 你用颤抖的嘴唇抿住茶碗口沿，任凭液体奔腾入胃，如同远古的海浪冲击着礁石，带给你一点小小的味觉震撼。
t_en: You press the teacup to your trembling lips and allow the water to rush into your stomach. The feeling of ancient ocean waves crashing against rocks sends a slight shock over your taste buds.

t_ch: 仅用好喝已不足以形容这碗茶，如果有「茶道」命途，你相信杜老板已经接近这条命途的终点，除了稍微有一丢丢咸……
t_en: "Delicious" is not enough to describe this tea. If there were a Path of "the way of tea," you are certain that Boss Du would be approaching its absolute pinnacle. If only the salty flavor could be slightly toned down...

t_ch: 怎么样？是不是很特别。
t_en: What do you think? Isn't it special?

t_ch: 确实特别，你是怎么做到的？
t_en: It is definitely special. How did you make this?

t_ch: 好喝，但你是不是放了盐？
t_en: It's delicious. But did you put salt in it?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这个配方可是本店绝对机密，你可千万别说出去。
t_en: Hehe, that recipe is a store secret. You mustn't tell anyone!

t_ch: 关键就在于泡茶用的水，寻常的井水河水都不行，必须用海水，而且还得是波月古海的海水。
t_en: The key is in the water used to brew the tea. Regular well or river water aren't good enough for this recipe. It has to be seawater. More specifically, it has to be seawater from the Lunarescent Depths.

t_ch: 为了保证茶的品质，我每天一大早就派机巧鸟从波月古海边偷偷汲水。最近那边似乎有所察觉了…这茶怕是有段时间喝不到喽。
t_en: In order to ensure the quality of the tea, I send cycranes to secretly collect water from the edges of the Lunarescent Depths first thing every morning. I think they're starting to catch on to me... I'm afraid this tea will be unavailable for a while.

t_ch: 波月古海。这四个字勾起了你的思绪——
t_en: The Lunarescent Depths. Those two words take you back...

t_ch: （向杜老板讲述你的见闻）
t_en: (Tell Boss Du about your experiences)

t_ch: 你回忆起在波月古海边捡漂流瓶的那段阳光灿烂的日子。
t_en: You recall the halcyon days when you would collect Drift Bottles along the Lunarescent Depths' shores.

t_ch: 可惜，跃渊还没有回复你漂流瓶的后续，不知道她那边现况如何了。
t_en: Unfortunately, Yueyuan still hasn't replied with an update about the Drift Bottles. You cannot help but wonder how things are going over there.

t_ch: （向小机器人询问那里的事）
t_en: (Ask the little robot about the situation)

t_ch: 小机器人向你解释了你的无名客伙伴们是如何在岸边捡漂流瓶的。
t_en: The little robot explains to you how your Nameless partners collected Drift Bottles on the shore.

t_ch: 很遗憾的是，地衡司尚未通过你的进入申请，也不知道此事的后续如何。
t_en: Unfortunately, the Realm-Keeping Commission still hasn't approved your application for entry. You have no idea what happened afterwards.

t_ch: 杜老板既是开茶庄的本地人，或许他会知道些什么。
t_en: Maybe Boss Du will know something about this as a local teahouse owner?

t_ch: 有没有在海边捡到过怪东西？
t_en: Have you ever picked up something strange while you were down at the shore?

t_ch: 怪东西？我想想…海带贝壳之类的倒是经常捡到。哦，我还捡到过一个漂流瓶。
t_en: Something strange? Let me think... I often pick up kelp, seashells, and things that get washed up by the water. Oh, I picked up a drift bottle once, as well.

t_ch: 那你肯定捡到过漂流瓶吧！
t_en: Then you must have picked up a Drift Bottle right?

t_ch: 你怎么知道的？我还真捡到过这么一个玩意。
t_en: How did you know that? I actually did find something like that...

t_ch: 当时我正准备打开看看，结果被一个巡逻的持明拦住，非说我是什么「放生帮」，我解释了半天她才放我走。
t_en: I was going to open it and see what was inside, but a patrolling Vidyadhara stopped me and kept calling me an "Unshackled" or something... I was explaining myself for hours before she let me go.

t_ch: 但是漂流瓶被她拿去了。唉…好想知道里面写了什么。
t_en: But she ended up taking the Drift Bottle with her. Ah... I'd love to know what was written inside.

t_ch: 胡编乱造的故事罢了。
t_en: Ah, it's all just a bunch of nonsense stories.

t_ch: 诶？你是怎么知道的。
t_en: Huh? How do you know that?

t_ch: 没准是求救信。
t_en: Maybe it was someone asking to be rescued.

t_ch: 如果是真的，那帝弓在上！保佑遇险者获救。
t_en: If that's true, then by the Arbiter! I pray that they will be rescued!

t_ch: 呀，跟您聊了这么久，都忘记招待其他客人了。你先自己品品茶，有什么需要尽管叫我。
t_en: Oh dear, I've been talking to you for so long that I've completely forgotten to serve my other customers! Please take your time tasting the teas. If you need anything, just call me.

t_ch: 我们和跃渊重新取得了联系，她给你发了短信，快看看吧。
t_en: I have managed to re-establish contact with Yueyuan. She has sent you a message. Check it quickly!

t_ch: 你看看信里写的东西，不知道这个写信人是脑洞太大还是日子太闲。
t_en: Here's what is written in the letter if you'd like to read it. I don't know if the writer's imagination has run away with them, or if they just have too much time on their hands.

t_ch: 看起来上一篇漂流记的故事已经结束连载，作者又开了一个新坑。
t_en: It looks like the previous story of being lost at sea has come to an end. It seems the author has taken a new artistic direction.

t_ch: 应该不会再有漂流瓶了。
t_en: I imagine that's the end of the Drift Bottles.

t_ch: 但愿如此吧，我受够折腾了。
t_en: I hope so. I've had enough of these twists and turns.

t_ch: 他转战科幻赛道了。
t_en: He's moved on to science-fiction.

t_ch: 恒星风气球…这人脑子里到底都有多少不切实际的想法？
t_en: A stellar winds balloon... Just how many ludicrous ideas can one person have in their head?

t_ch: 这回他糟蹋大海还不够，还想把垃圾倒进银河？
t_en: It wasn't enough for him to pollute the oceans, now he's throwing his trash into the Cosmos?

t_ch: 无论怎么说，这场闹剧终于告一段落，前段日子真是辛苦你了。
t_en: Whatever the case, it looks like this whole thing has finally come to an end. Thank you for your hard work back then.

t_ch: 不用再找我巡逻了吧？
t_en: You won't need me to go on patrols anymore?

t_ch: 啊？一句辛苦就完事了？
t_en: Huh? That's it? Just "Thank you for your hard work"?

t_ch: 以后就不用麻烦你了，我引进了一批机巧鸟，日常巡逻之类的任务都会交给它们去做。
t_en: I don't need to trouble you with all this anymore. I have bought a batch of cycranes that will do daily patrols and other missions like this.

t_ch: 当然啦，鉴于你为波月古海环境保护工作做出过卓越贡献，我为你向地衡司申请了一份特别奖励，应该很快就能通过审批。
t_en: Naturally, in recognition of your outstanding contributions to the environmental protection of the Lunarescent Depths, I have applied for you to receive a special reward from the Realm-Keeping Commission. The application should be approved soon.

t_ch: 待办事项已清空，可以准备返航。
t_en: All to-do items have been completed. You can prepare for your return when you are ready.

t_ch: 嘟嘟嘟噜——该回去充电咯。
t_en: Beep beep beep — it is time to return and charge my batteries.

t_ch: 噢？你要回去啦？那好吧，以后想来海边玩的话我随时欢迎。
t_en: Oh? You're leaving? Alright, if you wish to come and visit me at the seaside, you are welcome any time.

t_ch: 回来啦？看你的表情，想来收获不少吧？
t_en: You're back? That face of yours tells me that it was a worthwhile trip.

t_ch: 是的。此次旅程共记录了<unbreak>127</unbreak>条人类行为模式，<unbreak>2891</unbreak>条对话数据，以及<unbreak>46</unbreak>条情感波动记录。这些数据让我对{NICKNAME}与星穹列车有了更深的理解。
t_en: It was. During this trip, I recorded <unbreak>127</unbreak> human behaviors, <unbreak>2,891</unbreak> items of conversational data, and <unbreak>46</unbreak> instances of emotional fluctuation. This data has allowed me to gain a greater understanding of {NICKNAME} and the Astral Express.

t_ch: 她没问你。
t_en: She wasn't asking you.

t_ch: 我是你的随身助手，为你解决日常事务是我的职责。
t_en: I am your assistant. It is my duty to help you complete your daily affairs.

t_ch: 这家伙学得很快，挺有灵性。
t_en: This thing is pretty smart. It learns quick!

t_ch: 你说得我都有点不好意思了，我感觉我的芯片温度有点高。
t_en: Stop, you are embarrassing me. I feel like my processing chip is overheating.

t_ch: 说说看，这一路走下来，你有什么感受？
t_en: Well? What did you learn on this little adventure?

t_ch: 勿以善小而不为。
t_en: No good deed is too trivial.

t_ch: 感受到了「开拓」的意义。
t_en: I have learned what it means to "Trailblaze."

t_ch: 我刚开始做无名客的时候，也总想着要做些惊天动地的大事。拯救世界，改变命运…年轻人嘛，总是充满干劲。
t_en: When I first started as a Nameless, I always wanted to do great things, too. I wanted to save the world and change lives... You know what it's like as a youngster full of energy.

t_ch: 但慢慢地，我发现银河实在太大了。即便是最惊心动魄的壮举，在无垠的星海面前，也不过是沧海一粟。
t_en: But, over time, I discovered that the Cosmos really is just too big. Even the most earth-shattering events are no more than a drop in the ocean when it comes to the vast starry seas.

t_ch: 但与此同时，我们却在不断成长。无名客的一举一动，或许都能在普通人的生活里泛起不小的涟漪。
t_en: But we are always growing. Every action that a Nameless takes is capable of creating huge waves in the lives of normal people.

t_ch: 在旅途中播撒一些微小的光亮，给途经之处的人们一些微不足道的帮助，得到前进的勇气和希望的是他们吗？我觉得，受益的…似乎是我们无名客。
t_en: When we shine a little light along the way and help people as we travel, do you think it is they who receive more courage and hope? No. I think the ones that truly benefit... Are actually the Nameless.

t_ch: 正在存档…希望姬子小姐不要收取我录制你语句的版权费。
t_en: Saving... I hope that you will not charge me royalties for quoting you, Miss Himeko.

t_ch: 好像是这么回事。
t_en: I think you're right.

t_ch: 但这些小事又有谁记得呢？
t_en: But who will remember the small things that we do?

t_ch: 作为无名客，其实你已经做得很好了，永远不要低估自己的力量——来，给你看一样东西。
t_en: You are already doing an amazing job as a Nameless. Never underestimate your strength. Here, let me show you something.

t_ch: 姬子点开了小机器人的程序，同步了最新的数据。
t_en: Himeko accesses the little robot's programs and synchronizes it to the latest data.

t_ch: 我和工程师给这孩子做了些升级。现在它不仅能帮你处理更多事务，还学会了主动帮助他人。
t_en: The engineers and I gave this little one a few upgrades. Not only can it help you with more things now, but it has also learned to actively help others.

t_ch: 确认。已掌握{NICKNAME}的行为模式，今后将自主执行力所能及的善意行为。
t_en: Affirmative. I have studied and ascertained {NICKNAME}'s behavioral patterns and will independently carry out acts of kindness within my ability in the future.

t_ch: 它自己提出想升级新的通讯模块，这样就能方便你随时和这一路上认识的朋友们联系。
t_en: It suggested that it wanted to update its communication system to the latest model to make it easier for you to contact the friends you have met along your journey whenever you want.

t_ch: 通讯调试完成，已更新通讯人列表。
t_en: Communication system adjustment complete. Contacts catalog updated.

t_ch: 在更新完通讯模块之后，我们就立刻收到了这封感谢信。是仙舟地衡司的官方信函，旨在感谢你对波月古海环境保护工程的关心与认可。
t_en: When we updated the communication system, we immediately received this thank you letter. It is a formal letter from the Xianzhou's Realm-Keeping Commission, thanking you and recognizing your contribution toward the Lunarescent Depths environmental protection project.

t_ch: 除此以外，他们还寄了一大包特产过来。喏，这些全是给你的，小三月听说了你这一路奔波的事，坚持要把特产全都留给你。
t_en: They also sent over a huge pack of souvenirs. Here, they're all yours. When March heard about all your adventures, she was determined to save them all for you when you got back.

t_ch: 好啦，在外奔波这么久，接下来就好好休息吧。这个小家伙，依我看…可以放在派对车厢里，有什么事可以直接吩咐它去做。
t_en: Alright, you've spent enough time rushing around helping people. It's time to get some good rest. As for this little guy... Why don't you keep them in Party Car? If you have any issues, you can just ask them to take care of it for you.

t_ch: 有空的话可以跟它多交流交流，在升级了系统之后，它身上可是有不少惊喜功能等着你去探索呢。
t_en: Try talking with it whenever you get time. After the system upgrades, it now has lots of surprising functions waiting for you to discover.

t_ch: 有空的话可以跟它多交流交流，在升级了系统之后，它身上可是有不少惊喜功能等着你去探索呢。
t_en: Try talking with it whenever you get time. After the system upgrades, it now has lots of surprising functions waiting for you to discover.

t_ch: 雅利洛-Ⅵ——我们到了。
t_en: Jarilo-VI, we're here.

t_ch: ——还真是冰天雪地呀。
t_en: Brrr. It really is one big snowball.

t_ch: 还真是冰天雪地呀。
t_en: It really is one big snowball.

t_ch: 复读机啊你！
t_en: Hey, get your own metaphor!

t_ch: 也不是很冷嘛。
t_en: It doesn't feel that cold.

t_ch: 因为我们有来自「开拓」命途的微弱力量，能够适应严苛的环境。
t_en: Well the Trailblaze Path grants us faint power. It allows us to better adapt to harsh environments.

t_ch: 哎，这白茫茫的一片，咱们该往哪走？
t_en: *sigh* Snow as far as the eye can see. Which direction should we take?

t_ch: 根据坐标定位，目标就在前方不远处。
t_en: Based on the coordinates, the target should be up ahead.

t_ch: 那还等什么，这就出发吧～
t_en: Then what are we waiting for? Let's go.

t_ch: 何不直接降落在目的地？
t_en: Why didn't we land at the destination?

t_ch: …把泰科铵大球馆砸出个洞的事，你自己说还是我来？
t_en: ...Do you want to mention the time we smashed a hole through Taikiyan Stadium, or shall I?

t_ch: 呃，这事就别提了吧，反正着陆在没什么生物的地方就对了。
t_en: *sigh* Please stop bringing that up. Let's just say that landings and crowds don't mix.

t_ch: 除非你想体验义务劳动半个月的开拓之道。
t_en: Unless you enjoy trailblazing through two weeks of community service.

t_ch: 都说别提了嘛！
t_en: I said drop it!

t_ch: 居然还要步行一段路…
t_en: I didn't know we'd be hiking...

t_ch: 就是啊，连台备用的雪地车都没有——咱们的列车真抠门呀。
t_en: Me neither, if only we had a snowmobile... We never get to bring anything cool from the Express.

t_ch: 上一台雪地车被你开成什么样了，还记得吗？
t_en: Do you remember what you did to our last snowmobile?

t_ch: …跳过这个话题吧。
t_en: ...Anyway, moving on.

t_ch: 记住，迈出的每一步都务必谨慎，我们对于这个世界还所知甚少。
t_en: Remember, we should stay vigilant. We know very little about this world.

t_ch: 放心啦，有咱们三个在，什么事解决不了？
t_en: Calm down. Between the three of us, nothing will stand in our way.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}身体里有颗星核，我有独一无二的{RUBY_B#神秘的晶体}六相冰{RUBY_E#}，丹恒有…呃，不知道是啥的神秘过去。
t_en: I mean c'mon, {NICKNAME}, you've got a Stellaron in your body, I have my special {RUBY_B#Mysterious Ice Crystal}Six-Phased Ice{RUBY_E#} powers, and Dan Heng... Uhh, he's got that mysterious past thing going for him...

t_ch: 谁要是敢找我们的麻烦，算他倒霉！
t_en: So, if people start creating trouble for us, they're gonna regret it!

t_ch: 算他倒霉！
t_en: Yeah, they're gonna regret it!

t_ch: …复读的症状持续多久了啊？
t_en: ...So how long does "copycatitis" last?

t_ch: 我们别找麻烦就好…
t_en: Let's just make sure that we're not the ones creating trouble...

t_ch: 这个嘛，嘻嘻，咱可不敢保证～
t_en: Well, let's not get ahead of ourselves, hehe...

t_ch: 走吧，勇敢地探索未知——这就是「开拓」的精神！
t_en: Let's go. Braving the unknown... that's the real spirit of trailblazing!

t_ch: …喂，别躲啦，你冻得都打颤了。
t_en: ...Hey, get outta there or you'll shiver to death.

t_ch: 你忍着不出声也没用啊……
t_en: Holding your breath won't help...

t_ch: 让一下，三月。
t_en: I got this, March.

t_ch: 对付掩耳盗铃的人，最好的办法——
t_en: If someone's got their head stuck in the sand, or the snow in this case...

t_ch: ——就是把铃铛砸在他头上。
t_en: ...they just need a helping hand.

t_ch: 哎哟！
t_en: Yeowch!

t_ch: 我说哥们儿，不至于吧？钻雪里没啥大过错，不用拿枪尖子捅我吧？
t_en: My fine fellow, was that really necessary? Is crawling around in the snow a crime these days? I mean, c'mon, surely it doesn't warrant a spearing?

t_ch: 呃，但是，这怪不得各位英雄！哈哈，是我出现得太突兀了，挨这一戳值得，应当，必须！要不怎么能认识各位好朋友呢？哈哈……
t_en: ...But then again how can I blame you? I mean, I caught you off guard. It had to happen — you could even say I deserved it, huh? Besides, I've made a gallant group of new friends as a result, hehe...

t_ch: …请问杰帕德长官来了嘛？我跟他挺熟的……
t_en: ...Is Captain Gepard around? H—He's an old buddy of mine...

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 噢，你们不是银鬃铁卫？早说呀，自家人打自家人，这不是多此一举嘛！
t_en: Wait, you're not Silvermane Guards? Well, why didn't you say so? Turns out we're on the same side after all!

t_ch: 桑博•科斯基，幸会。
t_en: Pleasure to meet you, the name's Sampo Koski.

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 行，行，我记住了。说实话，没想到在这冰天雪地里能遇见同行。最近买卖不好干，不过你们放心，我桑博从不吃独食，外边这宝藏大得很，有财大家一起赚嘛，哈哈哈。
t_en: Excellent, I'll remember the name. I never thought I'd run into friends from the same line of work out here in this frozen wasteland. *sigh* Business is bad these days, but fear not, Sampo Koski isn't interested in hoarding. There's more than enough treasure to go around, so let's get rich together! Hahaha.

t_ch: 要不一起搭个伙呗？我有可靠消息：银鬃铁卫的主力都被调去前线了，这可是千载难逢的好机会……
t_en: Say, why don't we join forces? I have reliable intel the main strength of the Silvermane Guards is being deployed to the front line. This is a golden opportunity...

t_ch: 什么买卖？
t_en: What business are you talking about?

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫？
t_en: Silvermane Guards?

t_ch: 调去前线了？
t_en: The front line?

t_ch: …这就有点过了各位，不信任我没关系，但也不用装傻到这个地步嘛。
t_en: ...Come now, friends. I can understand the mistrust, but there's no need for the charade.

t_ch: 得，规矩我懂，大家都是干这行的，有点戒心，能理解！怪我桑博天性热情，太真诚……
t_en: Then again, I know the rules, vigilance is the name of the game in our profession. It's my fault for letting my enthusiasm and sincerity get the better of me...

t_ch: 相逢一场总归是缘分，各位有什么想了解的尽管问我，知无不言言无不尽！不过长话短说，银鬃铁卫随时可能出现……
t_en: Anyway, a meeting like this has to have been written in the stars. Ask me anything you like — I won't skimp on the details. Still, make it snappy, you're never more than ten feet from a Silvermane Guard...

t_ch: 聚居地在哪里？
t_en: Is there a settlement nearby?

t_ch: 要这么文绉绉吗，不就是哪有活人呗？你往外走是肯定没戏了，整个世界上唯一还有人类定居的地方，就只有咱们的好贝洛伯格啰。
t_en: Settlement? What a literary turn of phrase. Why, there's only one place in this world where the living still reside — our beloved Belobog! The further away you get, the dicier things become.

t_ch: 「存护之城」，「永屹之城」，「人类抵御寒潮的唯一壁垒」…听上去唬人，却句句属实。人类只有在这个铁围墙里才能活命。
t_en: The City of Preservation, the Towering Citadel, humanity's last bastion against the Eternal Freeze... It may sound a bit over the top, but those names are grounded in truth. The only place humans can eke out an existence is behind those impregnable walls.

t_ch: 你为什么钻雪里？
t_en: Why were you hiding in the snow?

t_ch: 我？还不是被你们吓到了…我本来在这儿东找找西翻翻，想搞点古代的遗物回去小赚一笔…结果你们发出那么大动静，我以为银鬃铁卫来了。
t_en: Me? You guys scared me to death... There I was looking for relics to sell, when all of a sudden you came stomping over. I thought the Silvermane Guards were paying me a visit.

t_ch: 算我拜托各位了，下次轻点，真的。铁卫碰见你们可不会躲雪里，你们呢？就得蹲牢里啦……
t_en: Seriously though, try treading a little lighter next time, hmm? If you run into the Guards, they won't hide in a snowdrift... and you'll be in a cell before you know it.

t_ch: 我真的不知道银鬃铁卫是什么…
t_en: I have no idea who these Silvermane Guards even are...

t_ch: …你们是真不知道啊？银鬃铁卫就是贝洛伯格的军队，执法者，警察…那帮家伙完全不懂变通，就喜欢和干咱们这行的人打交道。
t_en: You...You really don't know? The Silvermane Guards are Belobog's soldiers, enforcers, and police. Let's just say they're not the most flexible of people... and they like paying visits to folks in our line of work.

t_ch: 唉，诸位真的是新手啊…嫩成这样，我都有点看不下去了。这样吧，怎么说我也算是你们前辈，就免费给点指导好了。
t_en: Seems like you guys really are new to the business. *sigh* To be young and naive again... How about this. As a senior in the field, which I'm sure you don't mind me saying, I'll give you some free guidance.

t_ch: 干这行的有很多规矩，得好好教教你们：怎么潜行，怎么找货，怎么估价，怎么躲铁卫，那都是技术活……
t_en: There are ways of doing things in this profession, and you better get familiar with them. Moving in the shadows, finding the goods, pricing your stock, hiding from the Guards... there's an art to all of it.

t_ch: 那倒不必了，你带我们进城吧？我们对路也不大熟。
t_en: No need, why don't you just take us to the city? We don't really know the way.

t_ch: 进城？这就回去了？今天还没开张呢妹妹，带个路是小意思，但——
t_en: The city? Already? I haven't even started trading yet. Showing you the way is easy enough, missy, but it would cost—

t_ch: ——但我就是乐于助人。哈哈，心地善良就是我桑博的代名词！跟我来，朋友，轻点声，可别被铁卫发现了。
t_en: ...B—But it would be my pleasure! "Kindness" is Sampo Koski's middle name. Follow me, friends, and keep quiet. We don't want to be spotted by the Guards.

t_ch: …蓝头发的主犯呢？
t_en: ...And the prime suspect? The one with the blue hair?

t_ch: 抱歉，长官！跟丢了，我们找不到他的足迹……
t_en: Apologies, Captain! W—We lost him during the pursuit. We can't find his footprints...

t_ch: 不要紧，他的同伙在我们手里，主犯不会离得太远，一定会有所行动。
t_en: *sigh* No matter, we have his accomplices. He'll be close by, plotting his next move.

t_ch: 我们不是他的同伙。
t_en: We're not his accomplices.

t_ch: 呸，他也配！
t_en: Yeah, we'd never team up with someone like him!

t_ch: 那怕是要等上一辈子了。
t_en: You might be waiting longer than you think...

t_ch: ——都未必等得到。
t_en: ...As in, forever.

t_ch: 我可不是狡辩，我们跟那个坏蛋真不是一伙的。你看他丢下我们时哪有半点犹豫啊。
t_en: I'm not trying to talk our way out of this, but... we're not friends with that scoundrel. Did you see how fast he ditched us?

t_ch: 好心把他从雪里救出来，没想到他是利用我们来对付你。你可千万别受挑拨——
t_en: We rescued him from the snow out of the kindness of our hearts. We had no idea he might be using us to get past you! Are you really dumb enough to fall for his...

t_ch: 我是戍卫官，并非仲裁团。作为贝洛伯格的市民，你拥有辩护的权利，但那应该在筑城者的注视下进行，不是现在。
t_en: I'm a captain, not an adjudication panel. As a Belobog citizen, you have the right to defend yourself, but that can only take place under the scrutiny of the Architects, not now.

t_ch: 带他们回去。
t_en: Take them away.

t_ch: 可我们不是贝洛伯格的市民呀！
t_en: But we're not from Belobog!

t_ch: 我们穿的就和你们不一样。
t_en: Take a look at our clothes.

t_ch: 住口！你在说什么胡话——
t_en: Silence! What kind of nonsense are you—

t_ch: 不，也不能这么草率地下判断。你看他们的穿着，那确实不是贝洛伯格的风格。
t_en: As you were. We must not be tempted into careless judgment. Look at their attire. Indeed, it is not in the Belobog style.

t_ch: 对了，我还有照片！
t_en: Exactly! A—And we even have photos!

t_ch: 这些装备也不像你们的东西吧。
t_en: Does our equipment bear any resemblance to yours?

t_ch: 不，也不能这么草率地下判断。你看他们身上佩戴的那些古代遗物…我们从没发掘出类似的东西，博物馆里也没有相似的物件。
t_en: As you were. We must not be tempted into careless judgment. Look at their relics. We've never excavated anything like them before, and I haven't seen anything similar in our museums.

t_ch: 三月七，快给他看照片。
t_en: Show him the photos, March.

t_ch: 照片？
t_en: Photos?

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}你好聪明，这个点子棒耶！
t_en: Ah! {NICKNAME}, you're a genius! Great idea!

t_ch: 你们还没见过自己的星球长什么样子吧？给你们看我拍的雅利洛-Ⅵ……
t_en: You've probably never seen what your planet looks like, right? I took this one. Behold, Jarilo-VI...

t_ch: 三月七向杰帕德展示了这颗冰雪星球的照片……
t_en: March 7th shows Gepard a picture of the ice-covered planet...

t_ch: 你说这个白球是…这里？是我们住的地方？这也太……
t_en: ...You mean to say that this... white ball... That's here? That's our home? How can that...

t_ch: 据说很久以前，常有{RUBY_B#星际旅行者}天外异客{RUBY_E#}来到这里……但「寒潮」发生以后，就再没有人穿过雪幕，来到贝洛伯格……
t_en: It is said that a long time ago, {RUBY_B#Interstellar Travelers}strange visitors from beyond the sky{RUBY_E#} would visit us here... but that after the Eternal Freeze, the blizzards made passage impossible... and Belobog would cease to witness such arrivals.

t_ch: 但这几个人——
t_en: But these people are—

t_ch: 这已经不是我们能裁定的事了，如果他们所言属实，那就只有「大守护者」才能决定该怎么做。
t_en: This decision is beyond us. If what they say is true, then only the supreme guardian may decide their fate.

t_ch: 我们该做的，是将他们带到大守护者的面前。
t_en: Our job is to present them before her. Nothing more.

t_ch: 外来者们，跟我来吧。贝洛伯格就在这片雪幕背后。
t_en: Outsiders, follow me. Belobog lies beyond this blizzard.

t_ch: 欢迎来到「存护之城」——贝洛伯格。
t_en: Welcome to Belobog — the City of Preservation.

t_ch: 这里还没有被侵蚀，但裂界滋生的怪物已经跑出来了。
t_en: This place still hasn't been corroded, yet Fragmentum monsters have already made it here.

t_ch: 这世界里「星核」的影响恐怕非同寻常。
t_en: I fear the Stellaron may be exerting a significant influence on this world.

t_ch: 建筑已经被积雪掩埋了。
t_en: These buildings have been buried in the snowfall.

t_ch: 呜哇，这、这是房顶？这得要下多久的雪，才能积到这个厚度啊？
t_en: Huh!? So these are rooftops? How long would snow have to fall to get this thick?

t_ch: …很久很久。
t_en: ...A long, long time.

t_ch: …是虚卒的盔甲，很古老了。
t_en: This is Voidranger armor. It's ancient.

t_ch: 虚卒？这个星球被反物质军团入侵过吗？
t_en: Voidranger? Was this planet invaded by the Antimatter Legion?

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 哎，你们看见了吗？好像有什么东西在动……
t_en: *gasp* Did you see that? I think something's moving...

t_ch: …只是个寻常的雪堆，确定不是你的幻觉吗？
t_en: Mm... It's just an ordinary snowdrift. Are you sure you're not seeing things?

t_ch: 绝对不是，咱视力可好了！走，咱们靠近点儿看看。
t_en: Of course not, my eyesight's perfect! Come on, let's take a closer look.

t_ch: 所以，你为什么要躲着「银鬃铁卫」呀？
t_en: So why were you hiding from the Silvermane Guards?

t_ch: 呃，不过就是私藏了几件古代遗物而已，没什么大不了的。要怪就怪那帮执行公务的，脑子只有一根筋。
t_en: Uhh, I was just storing a few relics away from prying eyes, nothing serious. If it weren't for the uncompromising nature of our civil service, there'd be no need for secrecy.

t_ch: 说来，各位到底是什么来历啊？我可不是打听，就是关心关心朋友，不说也不要紧……
t_en: So whereabouts are you guys from, anyway? I don't mean to pry or anything, I just care about my friends! No pressure...

t_ch: ——第七条法则就是：不能留下脚印。我有条独门绝技，可以说踏雪无痕，专门用来摆脱追兵……
t_en: ...Rule number seven: Never leave a footprint. I have my own special technique called "invisible snow-walking." Helps me throw off pursuers in no...time...

t_ch: …这是？
t_en: ...Who are they?

t_ch: 呃，还记得我说的银鬃铁卫吗？就是他们了……
t_en: Uh, you remember the Silvermane Guards I mentioned? That's them...

t_ch: 帮个忙，哥们！我不想被抓啊！
t_en: Help me, old friends! I don't wanna be caught!

t_ch: 发现嫌疑人和他的同伙，立即逮捕！
t_en: It's the suspect and his accomplices! Arrest them!

t_ch: 此时不跑，更待何时！
t_en: It's now or never!

t_ch: 交给你们啦，朋友！
t_en: Over to you, dear friends!

t_ch: 喂！你这家伙——
t_en: Hey! Where do you think you're—

t_ch: 我，杰帕德•朗道，银鬃铁卫戍卫官，命令尔等放弃无谓的抵抗。
t_en: I, Gepard Landau, captain of the Silvermane Guards, order you to relinquish your futile resistance.

t_ch: 莫名其妙被那个桑博坑了一下，感觉很火大……
t_en: Grr, that Sampo cheated us all. Wait till I get my hands on him...

t_ch: 嫌犯！放弃抵抗，投降吧！
t_en: Suspect! Relinquish your resistance!

t_ch: 被当成罪犯，感觉更火大了！
t_en: Ugh, so I'm a criminal, huh? Forget Sampo, wait until I get my hands on YOU!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，三月七，这个杰帕德…很强。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, March, this Gepard... he's no pushover.

t_ch: 以「存护」的名义——
t_en: In the name of Preservation!

t_ch: （不跟紧这家伙的话，恐怕会迷失在雪雾里吧……）
t_en: (If we don't keep up with this guy, we might get lost in the blizzard...)

t_ch: （奇怪的物体…和这片雪原格格不入。）
t_en: (What a strange object... It seems really out of place in the Snow Plains.)

t_ch: （也许瓦尔特先生知道这是什么？发消息问问他吧。）
t_en: (Maybe Mr. Yang will know what this is. Let's send a message to ask him.)

t_ch: 说不动他呀…那就只有咱们两个人去咯？
t_en: He won't budge, huh? Looks like it's just the two of us then.

t_ch: 真可惜！好不容易能来一次不带包袱的旅行……
t_en: Pity, he's gonna miss out! We never get the chance to go on a stress-free adventure.

t_ch: 他在仙舟经历了太多。
t_en: He's been through a lot lately.

t_ch: 我当然理解他啦…这也不妨碍我觉得可惜嘛。
t_en: I know... But it's still a shame, don't you think?

t_ch: 你别乌鸦嘴啊…
t_en: Stress-free for us, or stress-free for the planet?

t_ch: 雅利洛-Ⅵ可是连星核都挺过去了，这回还能出什么大事？你就别操不必要的心啦。
t_en: Don't get yourself all worked up. If Jarilo-VI is strong enough to withstand a Stellaron, it'll be just fine.

t_ch: 唔，要是姬子能跟咱们一块去就好了……
t_en: If only Himeko could come with us...

t_ch: 不问问瓦尔特吗？
t_en: Should we ask Mr. Yang to come with us?

t_ch: 杨叔？杨叔跟咱们在仙舟大闹了一番，最近说话还连咳带喘的，你没注意到吗？
t_en: Mm, maybe not. He's still wheezing from the trouble we got into on the Xianzhou. You didn't notice?

t_ch: 而且他都这把年纪了，肯定不爱凑这个热闹啦……
t_en: At his age, he might not appreciate the extra... excitement.

t_ch: 帕姆呢？
t_en: What about Pom-Pom?

t_ch: 帕姆不能随便离开列车，它早就跟每个乘客都说过了吧？
t_en: Pom-Pom can't just leave the Express whenever they feel like it. You didn't know?

t_ch: 好啊，又抓住一个没听讲的！
t_en: Seems like someone wasn't paying attention!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，要不我们去问问姬子吧？没准她愿意跟咱们一起去呢！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why don't we go ask Himeko to tag along? She might be up for an adventure!

t_ch: 话说回来，你上车这么久了，还一次都没跟姬子搭档旅行过吧？这可不行——走，咱们去找她聊聊！
t_en: You've been on the Express for a while now, but you've never gone on a trip with Himeko before, right? Well, now's your chance!

t_ch: 丹恒这家伙，好歹也搭理布洛妮娅一下嘛……
t_en: That Dan Heng — he can't just ignore Bronya...

t_ch: 走吧，我们去他房间看看。
t_en: Let's go to his room and talk to him.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你听说了吧？咱们的下一站是「匹诺康尼」，听说那是个像梦一样漂亮的世界…真期待呀。
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, did you hear about our next stop? It's a world called "Penacony" — I hear it's a beautiful, dreamy place... I'm so excited...

t_ch: 不过帕姆说离邀请函上的赴会时间还差挺久的，该做点什么消磨时间呢……
t_en: ...but Pom-Pom said the banquet we were invited to is still a little ways off. Hmm... what can I do to pass the time...?

t_ch: …欸，手机响了？是群聊吗？
t_en: ...Huh? Is that my phone? Must be the group chat.

t_ch: 丹恒！布洛妮娅刚才给我们发消息啦，你看到了没？
t_en: Dan Heng! Bronya just sent all of us a message. Did you see it yet?

t_ch: 嗯，我看到了。
t_en: Yes, I saw it.

t_ch: 那你怎么不回话？布洛妮娅可是咱的老朋友欸！
t_en: Then why didn't you reply? Bronya's a good friend of ours!

t_ch: …抱歉，我只是没有闲谈的兴致。「罗浮」之后，我一直在反省和思考…这过程十分伤神。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, I'm just not in the mood for idle chat. There's been a lot weighing on my mind since the Luofu... it was tough on the spirit.

t_ch: 丹恒……
t_en: Dan Heng...

t_ch: 不好意思，我刚才说话太冲了！的确，在仙舟经历了那么多以后，你肯定需要些时间好好调整……
t_en: I'm the one who should be sorry — I was being too pushy! You're right, you went through a lot on the Xianzhou — take all the time you need...

t_ch: 丹恒就留在列车休整吧。
t_en: Dan Heng, stay on the Express and get some rest.

t_ch: 感谢理解，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you for understanding, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不如一起下去换个心情？
t_en: Why not come with us for a change of scenery?

t_ch: 还是不了，我就留在列车上吧。
t_en: No, thanks — I'll be staying on the Express.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格能够迎来节庆可谓一大喜事，祝你们不虚此行。记得替我向各位老友问好。
t_en: It's great that Belobog is able to host a celebration. I hope your trip goes well — say hi to our friends for me.

t_ch: 好！那你也要好好休息，可不要再错过下次「开拓」啦！
t_en: Okay! Rest up — you're not allowed to miss the next Trailblaze expedition!

t_ch: 嗯，一定。
t_en: Mm, I'll be there next time.

t_ch: 与此同时，在铆钉镇……
t_en: Meanwhile in Rivet Town...

t_ch: 收集了更多居民的意见后，最后的结果依旧是平票…
t_en: After collecting more citizen opinions, the final result remains a tie...

t_ch: 大矿区，开拓者和希儿这边……
t_en: Meanwhile, in the Great Mine with Seele and the Trailblazer...

t_ch: 与此同时，托帕和史瓦罗一边…
t_en: Meanwhile, with Topaz and Svarog...

t_ch: 怎么样，{NICKNAME}？我的新礼服，感觉如何？
t_en: So? What do you think about my new dress, {NICKNAME}? Pretty neat, right?

t_ch: 什么时候买的？
t_en: When did you buy it?

t_ch: 一直都在我的衣柜里啦，只是之前没机会穿出来。
t_en: It's actually not new, I just never had the right occasion to try it on.

t_ch: 盛装出席啊~
t_en: Very fancy!

t_ch: 哼哼…这件一直躺在我的衣柜里，这下总算有机会穿出来了！
t_en: It's been sitting in my wardrobe collecting dust for far too long. But now I've finally got the chance to wear it!

t_ch: 好不容易碰上一次这种场合，不好好打扮一番怎么行？倒是你，不会真的只有这一套衣服吧……
t_en: This kind of event doesn't come around every day — why shouldn't I dress for the occasion? ...You're not really gonna wear that same old outfit... are you?

t_ch: 算了算了，当我没说！咱们中间只要有一个注重仪表就够啦。
t_en: Ugh... let's just pretend I never said anything. As long as one of us is making an effort.

t_ch: 我看看…睡衣、保暖袋、还有帕姆的玩偶…齐活啦，准备出发！
t_en: Pajamas, hot water bottle, Pom-Pom doll... That should be everything! Let's go!

t_ch: 话说回来，你怎么收拾得这么快？没有什么要带的行李吗？
t_en: Wow, that was fast... Don't you have anything you wanna bring with you?

t_ch: 没什么要带的。
t_en: Nope, just myself.

t_ch: 对哦，感觉你每次都是轻装上阵…等等，你不会连睡觉都穿着这身衣服吧？！
t_en: Huh, you do seem to travel light. But... don't you at least need some pajamas!?

t_ch: 你带帕姆玩偶干嘛？
t_en: Why are you bringing Pom-Pom doll?

t_ch: 因为帕姆不能离开列车啊…所以只能用这种方法假装带它一起「开拓」过了。
t_en: Well, I thought since Pom-Pom can't leave the Express, bringing a doll with us is kind of like taking them trailblazing.

t_ch: 我的遗器带的都是速度词条。
t_en: My relics all have speed attributes.

t_ch: 我才不信呢，别想唬住我！
t_en: Other people might fall for that, but I won't!

t_ch: 雅利洛-Ⅵ…我刚才瞄了一眼杨叔发布的观测记录，相比我们第一次开拓之行的时候，那颗星球的平均地表温度已经回暖了不少。
t_en: I was just taking a look at Mr. Yang's records, and it seems that Jarilo-VI's average temperature has warmed up quite a bit. It's much warmer than it was on our first trip there.

t_ch: 虽然还谈不上宜居吧…但至少已经开始显出好转的迹象了，真替布洛妮娅跟希儿她们高兴。
t_en: I wouldn't call it liveable just yet, but things are looking up. I'm so happy for Bronya and Seele.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，怎么样？你准备好出发了嘛？
t_en: Ready to go, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 出发吧，过节去！
t_en: You bet! It's time to celebrate!

t_ch: 好耶！贝洛伯格，准备好再迎接本姑娘一次吧！
t_en: Belobog, here we come!

t_ch: 其实我还忘了点东西…
t_en: Actually, I think I forgot something...

t_ch: …什么呀，你这不是还没准备好吗？
t_en: What? You're still not ready to go?

t_ch: 快去快去，我就在这儿等你。
t_en: Alright slowpoke, I'll wait for you here.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，怎么样？你准备好出发了嘛？
t_en: Ready to go, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 出发吧，过节去！
t_en: You bet! It's time to celebrate!

t_ch: 好耶！贝洛伯格，准备好再欢迎本姑娘一次吧！
t_en: Belobog, here we come!

t_ch: 其实我还忘了点东西…
t_en: Actually, I think I forgot something...

t_ch: …什么呀，你这不是还没准备好吗？
t_en: What? You're still not ready to go?

t_ch: 快去快去，我就在这儿等你。
t_en: Alright slowpoke, I'll wait for you here.

t_ch: 雅利洛-Ⅵ…贝洛伯格……
t_en: Belobog... <unbreak>700</unbreak> years...

t_ch: 欠债还钱——
t_en: It's never too late...

t_ch: ——七百年不晚。
t_en: ...to pick up the tab.

t_ch: 活在风雪中的人们是怎么过节的呢……
t_en: Brr! How do people living in all this snow celebrate anything...?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不管哪个世界的节日庆典，肯定都少不了各种美食吧？
t_en: One thing's for sure though, {NICKNAME}. Where there's a celebration, there's bound to be food, right?

t_ch: 求求了，贝洛伯格…可别让我失望啊。
t_en: Come on Belobog... don't let me down.

t_ch: 停，{NICKNAME}！你看那边站着的人，穿的好像不是银鬃铁卫的军服？
t_en: Wait a sec, {NICKNAME}! That person over there. That doesn't look like a Silvermane Guard uniform...

t_ch: 那套黑色的制服，难道是…<color=#dbc291ff>「公司」</color>的人？在这个星球？不会是我眼花了吧？
t_en: All in black, surely they're not... <color=#dbc291ff>IPC</color>? On this world? Am I going crazy?

t_ch: 「公司」？
t_en: IPC?

t_ch: 还能是谁，当然是「星际和平公司」啦，就是到处都能看到它标志的那个「公司」——
t_en: The Interastral Peace Corporation — who else? The one whose insignia is pretty much everywhere? That IPC!

t_ch: …还没想起来？「查宝」你总记得吧？它就是公司的产品呀。还有…哦对了，「星际和平播报」！这个你总有印象吧，就是列车上总在放的那个！
t_en: You still don't remember? What about "Findie"? That's one of their products... Oh and the Interastral Peace Broadcast! Y'know, the one always being played on the Express!

t_ch: …算了算了，不重要。关键是，公司的人突然跑来雅利洛-Ⅵ干嘛？
t_en: Never mind, it's not that important. The important question is: What are the IPC doing on Jarilo-VI?

t_ch: 他们来这里干嘛？
t_en: What are they doing here?

t_ch: 我也在好奇…不会是来找「查宝」的吧？那他们可真是晚了好几步。
t_en: That's what I'd like to know. If they're here to look for Findie, then they're more than just a little late.

t_ch: 好丑的制服。
t_en: What an ugly uniform.

t_ch: 欸，是吗？我怎么觉得还挺酷的……
t_en: ...You think so? I actually think it's pretty snazzy.

t_ch: …这不是重点啦！关键是，公司的人突然跑来雅利洛-Ⅵ干嘛？
t_en: Anyway, that's besides the point! The point is, what are the IPC doing here on Jarilo-VI?

t_ch: 没办法，咱们主动上去问问吧？我印象里的公司员工态度都还挺好的。
t_en: There's only one way to find out — let's go ask. I've always found IPC staff easy to talk to.

t_ch: 哇…人声鼎沸呀！这还是我记忆里的贝洛伯格吗？
t_en: Wow! There are people everywhere. Are you sure we're in Belobog?

t_ch: 快，{NICKNAME}！快去看看广场上有什么好玩的、好吃的！
t_en: Now to check out the main event — the food! C'mon {NICKNAME}, keep up!

t_ch: 哈~累了，没想到什么也不干，光是闲逛也能这么消耗体力……
t_en: Whew... who'd have thought just taking a stroll could be so tiring?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，咱们是不是该去见见布洛妮娅？毕竟是她给咱们发的邀请函嘛。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, say, I think we should probably pay Bronya a visit. After all, she was the one who invited us here.

t_ch: 或者，咱们也可以再去别处逛逛，玩玩…全看你心情！
t_en: Or... we could go check out some other fun stuff elsewhere. Your call!

t_ch: 布洛妮娅，没事吧？那个托帕…居然有那么强势的一面，本来以为是个很好说话的人呢。
t_en: Is everything all right, Bronya? I didn't know Miss Topaz was so... intense. She seemed nice when we met her.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，三月…欢迎你们回来。抱歉，我本打算盛情迎接两位，但那位自称「公司」使者的女士突然出现，打乱了我的思绪……
t_en: It's good to see you two again, welcome back to Belobog. I'd planned a special reception, but I had to deal with an unexpected guest — the lady you just witnessed. She claims to represent the IPC...

t_ch: 既然是和你们两位，那我也没什么好遮掩的…老实说，我现在已经没什么精力思考煦日节的事了。
t_en: There's no need to keep either of you in the dark. To tell the truth, I've too much weighing on my mind right now to even think about the Solwarm Festival.

t_ch: 托帕到底和你说了什么？
t_en: What exactly did Miss Topaz say to you?

t_ch: 我听到了「债务」两个字…
t_en: I only heard the word "Debt."

t_ch: …嗯，既然你们之前已经打过照面，那我也不必说明她的身份了。
t_en: Mm... Since you've already made her acquaintance, I needn't explain who she is.

t_ch: 你们第一次来到贝洛伯格的时候，肯定听人提到过那段历史吧？有关七百多年前的军团入侵、还有因「星核」而生的寒潮……
t_en: I'm sure you heard pieces of Belobog's history the first time you visited — the Legion's invasion <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago, how the Stellaron brought about the Eternal Freeze...

t_ch: 现在看来，当时有许多重大的历史事件被埋藏在了风雪之下…那些记忆甚至没能随着历任大守护者传承下来。
t_en: It seems that much of Belobog's history from that time was buried deep beneath the snow. Those memories were lost and weren't passed down to the subsequent generations of Supreme Guardians.

t_ch: 居然还有这种事！所以…所以，难不成托帕说的「业务」，就是来贝洛伯格讨债？
t_en: Wow, that's a pretty incredible story. So... does that mean Topaz's "business" here is to collect a debt from Belobog?

t_ch: 嗯…恐怕是的。
t_en: Yes... I'm afraid so.

t_ch: 这也太离谱了吧？！早不来晚不来，怎么非挑这个时候？要是咱们没铲除掉星核，这笔账还不是就这么算了？这不明摆着是欺负人嘛！
t_en: That's ridiculous! And what's with the timing? What took them so long to get here? If it wasn't for us eliminating the Stellaron, they'd have nothing to collect! It's obvious they're just trying to take advantage.

t_ch: 根据她的说法，筑城者和公司投资人商定的还款年限是…<unbreak>280</unbreak>年。但借款之后不到百年，雅利洛-Ⅵ和整个银河的联系就彻底切断了。
t_en: According to her account, the repayment period agreed upon by the Architects and the IPC investors was... <unbreak>280</unbreak> years. However, less than a century after the loan was taken out, the connection between Jarilo-VI and the rest of the galaxy was completely severed.

t_ch: 公司认为雅利洛-Ⅵ遭受了毁灭级别的打击，文明已经不复存在，那笔投资也就被评定为了烂账。
t_en: The IPC deemed that the disaster had caused irreparable damage and that there was no civilization left to reclaim the debt from. As such, they considered the owed sum permanently lost...

t_ch: 直到「星核」危机解除，公司才发现贝洛伯格居然一直存续到了现在……
t_en: ...It wasn't until the Stellaron crisis was resolved that the IPC discovered that Belobog had survived...

t_ch: 然后他们就派了托帕过来催债……
t_en: And that's when they sent Topaz over to collect the debt...

t_ch: …嗯，连本带息。
t_en: ...And the interest.

t_ch: 数额是多少？
t_en: How much is owed?

t_ch: 天文级别…我甚至不知道该怎么准确地念出那串数字。
t_en: An astronomical amount. I don't even know how to read such a long string of numbers.

t_ch: 拖着不还会怎么样？
t_en: What happens if you don't pay it off in time?

t_ch: 那笔债务的数额…是天文级别，我甚至不知道该怎么准确地念出那串数字。
t_en: The debt owed is an astronomical amount. I don't even know how to read such a long string of numbers.

t_ch: 你们还没进来之前，托帕已经警告过我了…如果债务拖欠太久，公司给了她采取「强硬措施」的权力。
t_en: Before you both arrived, Topaz warned me that if payments were delayed, the IPC would give her the authority to employ "strong measures."

t_ch: 没想到事态会朝这个方向发展…我原本还以为，公司是来帮助贝洛伯格重建的……
t_en: I had no idea things were like this. Here I was thinking the IPC had come to rebuild Belobog, not extort it!

t_ch: 看来我们又得介入了。
t_en: Looks like we'll have to intervene.

t_ch: 是呀，咱们得帮布洛妮娅想想办法！公司的势力那么大，光靠贝洛伯格人自己肯定搞不定这事……
t_en: Yeah! We have to help Bronya out! The IPC is too big and powerful — the Belobogians won't be able to resolve this problem on their own.

t_ch: 只靠贝洛伯格人恐怕搞不定…
t_en: I doubt the Belobogians can do anything about this on their own...

t_ch: 是呀，咱们得帮他们想想办法！公司的势力那么大，就算是布洛妮娅出手也很难搞定吧……
t_en: Right, we need to figure out how to help them. The IPC is too big and powerful — even if Bronya steps in, it'll be hard for her to resolve the situation...

t_ch: 我对「星际和平公司」的了解没有你们多，但如果典籍中的记录属实…那是一个横跨整个银河的庞大组织，他们想镇压住贝洛伯格恐怕易如反掌……
t_en: You seem to have a deeper knowledge of the Interastral Peace Corporation than I do, but if the records are accurate and they truly have a presence throughout the galaxy, the IPC could crush Belobog like a bug if they wanted to.

t_ch: 所以…所以我很庆幸你们来了——我知道两位原本只是想来参加节日庆典的，但是现在……
t_en: I'm glad you're both here — I know you came for the festival, but now...

t_ch: …现在我想向你们，向星穹列车求救。两位，你们能否尝试替我说服托帕？借助列车在银河中的声誉，也许……
t_en: ...Now I must ask you and the Astral Express for help. Do you think you could persuade Topaz to reevaluate the situation? The Express' reputation precedes it, perhaps there's still hope...

t_ch: 放心吧，布洛妮娅！朋友有难，热心的无名客怎么可能拒绝？
t_en: Have no fear, Bronya! When a friend's in need, the Nameless will always be at their side!

t_ch: 我们去找她聊聊。
t_en: Let's go have a chat with Topaz.

t_ch: 嗯，毕竟{NICKNAME}可擅长谈判啦。
t_en: Yeah, after all, {NICKNAME} is a pretty good negotiator.

t_ch: 必要的话，武力取胜。
t_en: Let's bring out the big guns.

t_ch: 呃…倒是没必要闹得那么僵啦，托帕小姐看上去也不是那么不讲理的人。
t_en: Uh... I don't think we're at that stage yet. Miss Topaz seems like a reasonable person.

t_ch: 有{NICKNAME}的三寸不烂之舌，我们去试着说服她吧。
t_en: With {NICKNAME}'s silver tongue, I'm sure we'll make some progress.

t_ch: 谢谢你们！又要把你们卷入麻烦里了，实在抱歉……
t_en: Thank you so much, both of you. I'm so sorry for dragging you into more trouble.

t_ch: 两位去找托帕的这段时间，我也会思索一下应对的策略。祝你们好运——啊，还有，一定要注意安全。
t_en: While you're out looking for Topaz, I'll see if I can think of a strategy to deal with the situation. Best of luck, and stay safe.

t_ch: 这下知道托帕在哪了，我们快去博物馆找她吧。
t_en: Okay, got it. She's at the museum — let's get going.

t_ch: 咱们可得提前想想话术…年纪轻轻能在公司做到总监的位置，绝对是个人精！
t_en: We better think about what we're gonna say. She might be young, but she is an IPC executive — she's definitely no pushover.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…又把你们卷进贝洛伯格的事务，我很抱歉。但面对「星际和平公司」这种背景强大的势力，我们需要一切能够争取到的外部援助。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I apologize for dragging you into Belobog's affairs again, but when faced with an entity as powerful as the IPC, we need all the external help we can get.

t_ch: 两位去找托帕的这段时间，我也会思索一下应对的策略。祝你们好运——啊，还有，一定要注意安全。
t_en: While you're out looking for Topaz, I'll see if I can think of a strategy to deal with the situation. Best of luck, and stay safe.

t_ch: 托帕为何而来，能再讲一遍吗？
t_en: Can you tell me again why Topaz is here?

t_ch: 托帕小姐给我讲了一个故事：七百年前，星核降落于雅利洛-Ⅵ之后，原本在这个世界驻留的外来者预见到了危机的迫近，纷纷选择离去。
t_en: Topaz shared with me the story of the Stellaron's descent upon Jarilo-VI — how the original outsiders stationed here foresaw the impending disaster and chose to leave in large numbers.

t_ch: 这些外来者中就包括了一批「星际和平公司」的投资人。但他们在临走前做了一件大事——
t_en: Among those outsiders were several investors from the Interastral Peace Corporation. Before they departed, they did something noteworthy...

t_ch: ——他们找到了坚持驻守家园的「筑城者」，并向后者提供了一大笔资金，用于灾难避险。
t_en: They sought out Architects who refused to leave — those who insisted on defending their homes. The IPC provided these Architects with significant funds to aid them against the coming disaster.

t_ch: 正是凭借那笔资金，贝洛伯格才得以建起如此高大的城墙…甚至连最初一批自动机兵的研发经费都是从那笔钱里划出来的。
t_en: It was with those funds that Belobog was able to construct its immense walls... Even the initial funding for the development of the first batch of automatons was drawn from the same pool.

t_ch: 根据她的说法，筑城者和公司投资人商定的还款年限是…<unbreak>280</unbreak>年。但借款之后不到百年，雅利洛-Ⅵ和整个银河的联系就彻底切断了。
t_en: According to her account, the repayment period agreed upon by the Architects and the IPC investors was... <unbreak>280</unbreak> years. However, less than a century after the loan was taken out, the connection between Jarilo-VI and the rest of the galaxy was completely severed.

t_ch: 公司认为雅利洛-Ⅵ遭受了毁灭级别的打击，文明已经不复存在，那笔投资也就被评定为了烂账。
t_en: The IPC deemed that the disaster had caused irreparable damage and that there was no civilization left to reclaim the debt from. As such, they considered the owed sum permanently lost...

t_ch: 直到「星核」危机解除，公司才发现贝洛伯格居然一直存续到了现在…于是就派了托帕小姐前来催债。
t_en: ...It wasn't until the Stellaron crisis was resolved that the IPC discovered that Belobog had survived...

t_ch: 我们会尽力搞定这件事。
t_en: We'll do our best.

t_ch: 谢谢，{NICKNAME}。我相信你们。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. I believe in you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，托帕之前不是给了我们她的号码吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I just remembered Topaz gave me her number.

t_ch: 要不干脆直接点，发消息问问她在哪吧？她看上去像是喜欢有话直说的类型。
t_en: We might as well just be direct and ask her where she is. She seems like the type of person who appreciates a straight approach.

t_ch: 六百年前的画作……
t_en: <unbreak>600</unbreak> years old...

t_ch: 临时估值二十七万……
t_en: Provisional evaluation would value it at approximately <unbreak>270,000</unbreak>...

t_ch: 这样的画有三十四幅……
t_en: With <unbreak>34</unbreak> of this type of painting...

t_ch: 那就是九百一十八万……
t_en: That comes to <unbreak>9,180,000</unbreak>...

t_ch: …哎呀，居然又是二位！咱们这是什么神仙缘分，一天里居然能撞上三次？
t_en: Oh! It's you two again. This is the third time today we've run into each other.

t_ch: 珍惜这段缘…
t_en: Cherish the moment...

t_ch: 我还是第一次认识星穹列车的朋友，当然得好好珍惜啦。
t_en: Why, of course I will! Meeting friends from the Astral Express is always a moment worth cherishing!

t_ch: 其实我们给你发了消息。
t_en: Actually, we sent you a message.

t_ch: 哦，是智能回复告诉你们我在这儿的？这员工定位系统是「技术研发部」负责开发的，是不是挺厉害的？
t_en: Oh, did my Smart-Reply function tell you where I was? It's a system developed by the Technology Department. Pretty neat, right?

t_ch: 你们看过这幅画吗？我虽然不太懂艺术鉴赏，但大概也能从画家的笔触里看出些东西。
t_en: What do you make of this painting? I'm no expert, but just from the artist's strokes I can tell it's no ordinary piece.

t_ch: 该怎么说呢？整幅画面都传递着一种…悲伤。不是普通人遭遇不顺心时那种短暂的伤感…远比那种感觉更悠长，更厚重。
t_en: It portrays a certain kind of... sorrow, you know? Not your everyday kind of sadness — it runs much deeper than that.
